text:
Once upon a time, a boy named Toby lived with his parents near a forest filled with ancient trees whose branches reached down into pools of sunlight they had never seen.

Every day after school, Toby would sneak out of his house to visit the forest. He loved the way the leaves whispered secrets and the way the rustling sounds like music made him feel special. Sometimes he would sit under a big owl tree, listening to its soft chuckle that sounded just like his own funny thoughts.

One day, Toby found a strange, shiny object on the forest floor. It glittered like stars. He picked it up eagerly. It was a magic mirror. When he looked into it, he saw his reflection not just as a boy, but as different things – like a bird soaring through the trees, a river gliding slowly, and leaves dancing.

He spent hours exploring this magical place.
